hero.headline: PESO License in India – Online Registration, Fees, Process & Renewal
hero.subtext: Legal compliance, safety approvals, and end-to-end licensing support for hazardous goods.

introduction.heading: Introduction and its Compliance
body: PESO License is a statutory licence notified by the Petroleum and Explosives Safety Organization (PESO), an organization working under the Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT), Ministry of Commerce & Industry, Government of India. The license is mandatory for an individual or entity which is involved in manufacturing, keeping, treatment, transport, sale, in any form and under any name of petroleum products, explosive and compressed gases, fireworks, flammable substances. The purpose is to ensure public safety, industrial risk management, and legal compliance pursuant to the Explosives Act, 1884 & its Rules made under it. As we have indicated; the PESO License requires strict compliance to safety standards, approvals for designs/ equipment’s and timely checks. Professionals Law helps businesses get PESO approvals faster, namely guiding them across the technicalities to reduce legal risk and safely operate within the regulatory framework.

whyNeeded.heading: Why It Is Needed
body: The PESO License is an essential legal permission for the businesses that want to engage in the activities such as storage, transportation, handling, and manufacturing of Explosive/Hazardous substances. It also ensures a high level of protection for human life, property and the environment against hazards and risks. This permit is required to operate such industries where the flammable material could include petro-chemical, natural gas or firecrackers and fire works, storage and processing of chemicals. Getting the PESO License not just helps in being a complaint under the law but also develops guidelines and control mechanisms in practice for safety and prevention of accidents or legal liabilities.

body.title: Ensures Legal and Operational Safety
body.description: A valid PESO license confirms adherence to safety norms, protecting workers, assets and nearby populations from accidents or hazardous leaks.

body.title: Mandatory for Industrial Clearances
body.description: The license is often a prerequisite for obtaining environmental NOCs, factory licenses and municipal clearances in industrial zones.

body.title: Enhances Credibility and Public Trust
body.description: PESO-licensed companies demonstrate regulatory compliance and safety awareness, improving their brand image and stakeholder confidence.

body.title: Avoids Legal Action and Operational Delays
body.description: With the proper license, businesses can avoid closure, seizure of goods or legal penalties from non-compliance with explosive and petroleum laws.

body.title: Streamlined Storage and Handling Approvals
body.description: It allows safe handling and transport of petroleum, gas cylinders and explosive substances with approved site plans and safety protocols.

criteria: Entities involved in manufacturing, storing or transporting petroleum, explosives or compressed gases.
The facility must be located and built as per safety distances and PESO site guidelines.
Applicants must be individuals or legally registered entities such as companies or partnerships.
Applicant must appoint a qualified safety officer (where required).
Necessary structural and safety infrastructure must be in place before applying.

documents: Identity and address proof of authorized signatory
Site plan and layout of storage/processing facility
Fire safety equipment list and installation details
Lease deed or ownership documents of premises
NOC from local authorities or fire department

body.description: Professionals Law identifies the appropriate license type based on the material handled (e.g., Form XI, Form XIV, etc.).
Preparation of layout and safety plan as per PESO norms.
Compilation and formatting of all supporting documents and safety certifications.
Submission on the PESO online portal (explosives.gov.in).
PESO officers inspect the facility for compliance with prescribed safety norms.
On successful inspection and scrutiny, the license is granted.

body.title: Penalties
body.description: Operating without a valid PESO license may lead to heavy fines, imprisonment, suspension of business or sealing of premises. Mishandling of hazardous substances without appropriate safety protocols can lead to criminal prosecution under the Explosives Act and Petroleum Act.
30 to 90 days, depending on the complexity of operations,

body.question: What does PESO stand for?
body.answer: PESO stands for Petroleum and Explosives Safety Organization, a regulatory body for explosive and petroleum safety in India.

body.question: Who needs a PESO License?
body.answer: Any individual or entity dealing in storage, sale, manufacture or transport of hazardous materials like LPG, petroleum, fireworks or industrial gases.

body.question: Is PESO license state-specific or national?
body.answer: The PESO license is centrally regulated but site-specific, meaning each operational facility requires separate approval.

body.question: What happens if I operate without a PESO license?
body.answer: It is a punishable offense with severe penalties, including closure of the unit, seizure of goods and criminal prosecution.

body.question: Is there a renewal requirement for PESO licenses?
body.answer: Yes, most PESO licenses are valid for 1 to 5 years and must be renewed within the due period to avoid penalties.

body.question: Do petrol pumps require a PESO license?
body.answer: Yes, petrol pumps must obtain PESO certification to ensure the storage and dispensing of fuel complies with safety norms.

body.question: Can Professionals Law help with renewal and modifications?
body.answer: Yes, Professionals Law offers assistance with PESO license renewal, amendments and safety audits.

body.question: Is factory license required before PESO license?
body.answer: For manufacturing units, a factory license and pollution NOC are typically required before or along with PESO application.

body.question: Are fire NOCs and environmental clearances also needed?
body.answer: Yes, for most high-risk sites, a fire department NOC and pollution clearance are necessary alongside the PESO application.
